name: Farmers Trust & Savings Bank
description_short: Family-owned community bank in southwest Iowa serving personal, agricultural, and business customers since 1892 with three full-service branches and modern digital banking.
description_long:
Farmers Trust and Savings Bank was founded in 1892 as the German Savings Bank in Earling, Iowa, by J. Carl Heese and local investors. The bank was renamed in 1917 to reflect its growing focus on supporting farmers, families, and businesses. Today it operates as a family-owned institution with Todd Langenfeld, the great-grandson of the founder, serving as Chairman of the Board. The bank marks 133 years of continuous operation as the oldest locally owned and operated financial institution in Shelby County.

The bank offers a comprehensive range of personal and business financial services across three full-service locations in Earling, Woodbine, and Harlan, Iowa. Services include personal and student checking and savings accounts, personal loans, auto loans, home loans, business loans, agricultural loans, and MasterCard debit cards. Customers have 24/7 ATM access at all three locations, and the bank provides online and mobile banking platforms enabling account management, fund transfers, bill payments, mobile check deposit, and real-time transaction alerts.

Farmers Trust and Savings Bank distinguishes itself through genuine local decision-making, personal service, and deep community roots. The bank explicitly positions itself as a place where customers are "a name not a number." With operations spanning over 130 years in the same region, the institution has developed specialized expertise in agricultural lending and understands the specific financial needs of rural communities. The bank emphasizes that it keeps local dollars working close to home and maintains family ownership rather than corporate chain management.

The bank's primary limitation is geographic reach—it operates only three branches in a limited area of southwest Iowa (Earling, Woodbine, and Harlan), making it inaccessible for non-local customers seeking in-person banking. While the bank offers digital banking tools, the website does not provide specific details about competitive rates, loan terms, account minimums, or fees. Prospective customers would need to contact the bank directly or visit in person to obtain detailed product pricing and terms. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: 24/7 ATM access at all three locations
pros: 133 years of continuous operation—longest-serving locally owned bank in Shelby County, Iowa
Family-owned and operated with founder's great-grandson as current Chairman, ensuring local decision-making
Specialized agricultural lending expertise tailored to rural farmers and operations
Three full-service branch locations with 24/7 ATM access at all sites
Modern digital banking including mobile app, online account management, mobile check deposit, and bill pay
FDIC-insured deposits backed by full faith and credit of U.S. Government
Personal service model with emphasis on knowing customers by name rather than account number
cons: Limited geographic footprint—only three branches in southwest Iowa (Earling, Woodbine, Harlan) with no online-only or national accessibility
Website lacks specific information on rates, fees, minimum balances, and loan terms—requires direct contact for pricing details
No mention of competitive products like CDs, money market accounts, investment services, or wealth management
best_for: Farmers and agricultural business owners in southwest Iowa seeking specialized ag lending and community banking relationships
Families and individuals in Shelby County, Iowa seeking personal banking with local decision-making and relationship-based service
Small business owners in rural Iowa communities looking for business loans with local understanding and personal service
diagnosis: Farmers Trust and Savings Bank is best for rural Iowa residents, farmers, and small business owners in Shelby County seeking personalized banking with genuine local decision-making and agricultural expertise. The primary caveat is strict geographic limitation—customers must be in or near Earling, Woodbine, or Harlan, Iowa to access branch services, and the bank does not serve customers online-only or outside its regional footprint.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
meta_description: Family-owned community bank in southwest Iowa since 1892 serving personal, agricultural, and business customers with three full-service branches.
